title: The Self-Sealing Slab
passage: A parking deck in the coastal district of Calder Vale has spent the past two years quietly repairing itself. Its concrete was mixed with an additive developed by Dr. Teodora Reyes and her team at the Halvorsen Institute for Applied Materials: millions of tiny clay capsules, each holding a mineral slurry. When a hairline crack spreads through the concrete and tears open a capsule, the slurry seeps into the gap, reacts with moisture in the surrounding air, and hardens into calcite, knitting the crack shut. The trigger, Reyes stresses, is the cracking itself — not a rise in temperature, and not any chemical brushed on afterward. In field tests the treated slabs sealed cracks up to half a millimeter across, though the additive does nothing for the larger structural fractures that only steel and engineers can address.

questions.prompt: According to the text, what makes the additive seal a crack in the concrete?

options.text: A rise in temperature that sets the mineral slurry stored inside the slabs.
The spreading crack itself, which tears open the capsules and frees the slurry.
A sealing chemical brushed onto the surface once the cracks become visible.
Moisture in the air, which slowly dissolves the clay capsules throughout the slab.

questions.rationale: TYPE-001 direct detail (paraphrase_one_sentence). The text states the slurry is released 'when a hairline crack ... tears open a capsule' and names the trigger explicitly: 'the trigger ... is the cracking itself'. B paraphrases that. A = contradiction/outside_knowledge: the text rules it out ('not a rise in temperature'). C = contradiction: the text rules it out ('not any chemical brushed on afterward'), and 'brushed onto the surface' also breaks the self-healing mechanism. D = half-right distortion / surface_word_match: moisture is mentioned, but as what the freed slurry 'reacts with' to harden — it does not 'dissolve the clay capsules', and it is not the trigger; the crack is.

fictional_source.byline: Dr. Teodora Reyes, invented materials scientist
